channel.title: KillBait RSS Feed
channel.description: KillBait - News highlights delivered clearly and responsibly—no clickbait, no sensationalism

item.title: Edo State Orders Arrest and Prosecution of Students Involved in School Bullying
item.description: Edo State government don order police to arrest and prosecute students wey dey involved for serious bullying wey go viral for Igbinedion Education Centre. According to Kassim Afegbua, the state Commis...

item.title: Atlassian employee dismissal raises questions about workplace free speech
item.description: Atlassian, the Australian software company known for tools like Jira and Trello, is facing scrutiny over the firing of software engineer Denise Unterwurzacher in June 2023. US labour authorities claim...

item.title: King Charles engages with young people in Manchester through music and arts programmes
item.description: King Charles III visited Manchester and Warrington as part of a day of engagements promoting The King’s Trust initiatives. At Aviva Studios, he met students benefiting from the Creative Futures and Ge...

item.title: Guide on How to File VAT Returns on iTax in Kenya to Avoid Penalties
item.description: VAT filing on iTax Kenya is a must-do for all businesses registered for value added tax. Every month, by the 20th of the following month, you have to submit your VAT return even if you had zero sales ...

item.title: Theatre Production Pays Tribute to Late Activist Fikile Ntshangase and Highlights Dangers Facing Environmental Defenders
item.description: A new theatre production titled Isitha Sabantu has opened at Johannesburg’s Market Theatre, paying tribute to murdered environmental activist Fikile Ntshangase. Ntshangase, who was killed in 2020 for ...

item.title: UNSC Extends UNAMA Mandate for Three Months Amid Security Concerns in Afghanistan
item.description: On Monday, the United Nations Security Council (UNSC) unanimously voted to extend the mandate of the United Nations Assistance Mission in Afghanistan (UNAMA) for three months, running from March 16 to...

item.title: The psychological impact of repeated exposure to war imagery
item.description: The ongoing conflicts in the Middle East, particularly the recent escalation involving Iran, the US, and Israel, are exposing audiences worldwide to repeated images of human suffering and violence. Re...

item.title: Privy Council Office budget set to rise under Carney with plans to reduce staff long-term
item.description: Prime Minister Mark Carney has pledged to reduce government waste and operational spending. However, recent federal documents show that the planned budget for the Privy Council Office (PCO) for 2026-2...

item.title: Chelsea receives record fine and temporary transfer bans for past rule violations
item.description: Chelsea football club has been fined a record 10.75 million pounds by the Premier League due to historical breaches of league rules. The club also faces a one-year first-team transfer ban, suspended f...

item.title: Philippine Health Authorities Call for Full Vape and Nicotine Product Ban to Protect Young People
item.description: During a Senate health committee hearing on March 16, 2026, the Department of Health (DOH) and health experts in the Philippines proposed a total ban on vapes, heated tobacco products (HTPs), and othe...

item.title: California and 14 States Challenge Trump Administration Over Fair Housing Rollback
item.description: California, along with 14 other states and the District of Columbia, filed a lawsuit against the Trump administration to prevent changes to federal housing rules that would weaken protections for LGBT...

item.title: Cardiff schools closed as police investigate online weapons threats
item.description: Two schools in Cardiff, Llanishen High School and Eastern High School, were closed after online threats suggested that an individual intended to attend the schools carrying weapons. South Wales Police...

item.title: Parliament urges timely payment to Aviation College contractor to ensure project completion and worker welfare
item.description: Dar es Salaam – The Parliamentary Standing Committee on Infrastructure has called on the government to ensure that workers’ rights are respected and that the contractor handling the Aviation Training ...

item.title: Whitemoney Reacts to Viral Video of Fiancée, But She Clarifies It’s Fake
item.description: Big Brother Naija Season 6 winner, Whitemoney, recently caused online buzz after a video clip showed his fiancée, Emmanuella Iloba, allegedly kissing actor Chike Daniels. Fans quickly reacted to the c...

item.title: HPV vaccination in early adolescence significantly reduces risk of cervical and other cancers
item.description: The HPV vaccine offers a powerful means of preventing certain cancers before they even develop. Typically administered to 12-year-olds through school programmes, the vaccine protects against the human...

item.title: AFC Confirms No Withdrawal Notification from Iran for 2026 FIFA World Cup Participation
item.description: The Asian Football Confederation (AFC) has clarified that Iran's men's national football team is still expected to compete in the 2026 FIFA World Cup, co-hosted by the United States, Canada, and Mexic...

item.title: Chelsea fined £10.75m for financial misconduct during Abramovich era; Premier League awaits Manchester City verdict
item.description: The Premier League has imposed a record £10.75 million fine on Chelsea for secret payments made from third-party entities to facilitate player signings during Roman Abramovich's ownership, spanning 20...

item.title: Middle East conflict escalates: Israel strikes Hezbollah positions and US seeks support for Strait of Hormuz
item.description: The conflict in the Middle East has intensified with Israel launching a broad wave of air and ground attacks against Iran-backed Hezbollah positions in Beirut and surrounding suburbs. Reports indicate...

item.title: Government Prepares to Launch 369km SGR Extension to Western Kenya
item.description: The Kenyan government is gearing up to start construction on the 369-kilometre extension of the Standard Gauge Railway (SGR) from Naivasha to Kisumu, eventually reaching Malaba. This project is a majo...

item.title: Eskom Announces 8.76% Tariff Hike for 2026/27, Subsidies Maintained for Vulnerable Households
item.description: Eskom, South Africa’s state-owned power utility, has received approval from NERSA to implement an average electricity tariff increase of 8.76% for the 2026/27 financial year. The increase will take ef...
